content:
I would like to start getting into d-tide now but only have two teams shown in the pictures
alongside it i have attached the rest of my awakeners 
was hoping to get some help on what other teams i could use for the time being just to get some more d-tide points and rewards
thread_title: D-Tide Teams

content:
Been about a week since i started, maybe a bit longer. I feel like there's a lot I'm not picking up on, partially because I tend to be a bit busy and wasn't sure if I would enjoy the game enough. For teambuilding, I've heard i want a damage dealer on my team with other members serving a more supportive role, so I'll skip that to ask for the deeper parts, namely:

1: how do you determine what units work with what wheels?

2: what decides the covenant i run? 

3: how to tell if i want dupes of a character?

I'm aware there are various guides telling me things like meta teams or covenants to run, but i want to better understand the "why?" portion
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content:
before replying, one thing I want to clarify is that a team centered around a dps is not the only way to build a team, albeit it tends to be the most straightforward one. If you check the section "Types of Teams" in Cheri's guide you'll see what I mean. Do keep in mind that some teams have prerequisites in order to perform well (stall having an enemy that does not scale super hard and an external source of damage, counter having an opponent with multistrikes, etc).

1: it usually boils down to having enough keyflare generation (usually from a dedicated keyflare bot), wheels that synergize with the dps of choice's source of damage or gameplan. For some awakeners, the main stat is also a factor (check their enlightments and talents to see if they scale off of an attribute. For example, Saya scales off sigil yield). I guess it's something that you learn as you get familiar with how your team operates. Also keep in mind that R wheels are really strong in arc 1, unlike in arc 2 where they are quite bad and keyflare becomes even more prevalent. My suggestion would be to check the Mythag Compendium (link below too) and check the tabs labeled for each realm. It has sugestions on wheels, so you can check what they do and understand from there how it benefits the awakener. There are also notes indicating further alternatives and how much the given awakener cares about their wheels.

2: There are a couple that are used quite often (Life Drain, Burial Ground, April Tribute, Dream of Medicine), but, once again, it boils down to what the awakener wants to do. Mythag compendium has covenant suggestions, and thee following covenant guide explains what these common covenants do and how to build them: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NipTUNwBwenon3CTBFB2CD1sy3ll6RFY3SjUqW8CkwI/edit?usp=sharing

Each Covenant gi...

thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content: 3: check what each enlightment and OE does, and determine what is mandatory or really beneficial to their gameplan. Again, Cheri's guide tells you what these breakpoints are. Also, at the end of the day this is subjective, people disagree about what is and what is not a breakpoint quite frequently. Make your own assessment.

content:
I'm currently running main team of Arachne(e0) + Murphy(e0) + GMurphy(e0) + Caecus(e2). Have been clearing the story quite smoothly with Arachne's skills up until mid chpt8-7 onwards where I needed to use emergency gnosis to kill the final boss.  (managed to clear until 8-10 at the moment)

Could I get some advice on what I should invest in to make my story team more robust? Should I be swapping out some members instead? 

For covenant, currently im keeper lvl40, waiting till im keeper lvl45 to farm for the general cov sets for the other 2 members.

Thank you in advance for any help with this!
thread_title: Need help for end Chpt 8 Story. Do I need to swap or reinvest in new members?

content:
First and foremost, level your cards to 3 minimum, it is super cheap to do and you're hurting your aliemus generation by not leveling strike/defends and rouse. Level your covenants and give one to your supports too (two Burial Ground's Sighs, for example, give a lot of DR, which is more survivability). Guide on covenants: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NipTUNwBwenon3CTBFB2CD1sy3ll6RFY3SjUqW8CkwI/edit?usp=sharing

 You've seen that ch7's boss loves to give status cards and spam mobs, so cycling cards and AoE works well. Honestly, I think giving the blade to gmurphy would be more beneficial, since red stance + exalt +waltz should work well for that purpose. I would also swap the posse for a mouse's wisdom and give actual keyflare wheels to the awakener with life drain. There are a couple SR wheels that are pretty good too, factor them in (like the one with the silver key icon, which gives aliemus on posse, or the one with a hourglass, which gives aliemus on playing symptoms). Make sure to do the first couple hard mode chapters, they should be trivial for you at this point and they give a lot of keeper exp, which translates to max hp.

thread_title: team building & who to invest in

content:
first, temper your expectations and tackle lower alerts until you have a more developed roster (that includes dupes). Leaving aside the 5th team, which be assembled from the leftovers and be carried by a whale borrow, I would move Saya out of Mouch's team and make a third team off of that with another dps (I'm thinking either Goliath, Jenkin or Sanga). Then, I would make either a poison or a counter team. You have options in this regard (Faros+Aurita+Arachne, Liz+Clem, Caecus+Arachne+Clem, poison teams with Lily or Xu, etc), just keep in mind that you might need dupes or additional support depending on what you are aiming for. Hameln and Tawil also work well with Vortice, although your Tawil is lacking in dupes.

Beyond this, ask in <#1400749543288475770> for more specific questions and check Cheri's guide and the Mythag Compendium.

thread_title: D-Tide Teams

content: So if i'm understanding the Saya example correctly, the choice of sigil yield wheels is because of her E1 specifically? Unless i missed an additional detail somewhere. And i'm assuming the covenant guide says Dream of Medicine to get more of her 0 cost, and Deus Ex Machina because of how much Aliemus she needs to exalt, and Feast From Afar for more sigil yield?
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content: yes, it's basically a big boost to her shield and corrrosion. and correct on the covenants, although dream is because both her 0 and 2 cost are very important for her gameplan. I don't fully see her using Feast From Afar, I guess it'd be because of the sigil yield + some of her teams use Ephemeral Enternity, although you'd want to generate her strike/defense if she's e2 tbh. Also consider Burial Ground if you have nothing else available and don't want to use Deus Ex.
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content: Then looking at another character, why would i want Sanga to have death resistance? Is it because there's nothing else to give? I don't think i saw anything suggesting she scales with it?
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content: her talent enlightment of death scales off death res, but it is not as big of a high prio, since the bulk of her damage comes from shield amount. I've seen Sangas run Celestial Beast, for example (as a dps).
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content: It is also not as relevant if you just want her for her shields, at that point run land of non-existence (SR wheel) and anything that will support the team
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content: Ah i see. Is it important to level my covenants if i'm just using them until i unlock a different one?
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content: +12 the main stat of each piece of the covenant. Do the universal ones from the covenant's guide
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content: you can worry about substats once you have more resources saved
thread_title: Request for a Look In a Teambuilder's Head

content:
for level 70, prioritize dps then tanks, with supports for last. Factor in how high their stats are, each awakener has different values.

Check Cheri's guide, since it has suggestions for teammates for each awakener, as well as a section talking about how to approach dtide (what alert you wish to do is a big factor on what works or not). I guess my suggestion would be to pick the 4 dps or themes around your teams, build them, then make a 5th team with a whale dps and whatever you have left that will help said borrowed awakener (Mouchette is a common pick due to not being restrictive when team building her, plus being plain broken).

You have plenty of options from what I can see (Mouch, Vortice, Castor+Jenkin, Saya+dps, Sorel, Poison, Corpo+Tulu discard, etc), look up which ones you might enjoy more and check the guide I mentioned earlier.
